---
title: "Size vs. Bd modeling: Panamá preliminary results"
author: Cob Staines
date: 2025-09-29
format:
  html:
    toc: true
df-print: kable
bibliography: references.bib
csl: ecology.csl
---
# Setup

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| output: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show setup code"

# setup
librarian::shelf(tidyverse, dbplyr, janitor, here, brms, tidybayes, arrow)

```

# Data selection

## environmental
```{r}
env_agg = read_parquet(here("data", "unified_env_agg.parquet"))

```

## RACA
```{r}
raca_clean = read.csv(here("data", "JPSL_RACA_Swabbed_Individuals_2026-03-03.csv")) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  filter(life_stage %in% c("A", "S", "Y", "M")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(site = site_name,
         body_mass_g = weight_g,
         site_utme = utme_site,
         site_utmn = utmn_site,
         bd_its_copies = bd_swab_results_its) %>%
  mutate(life_stage_simple = case_when(
    svl_mm >= 50 ~ "adult",
    svl_mm < 50 ~ "subadult"),
    date = as.Date(date),
    site = tolower(gsub(" ", "_", site)),
    site_utm_zone = 11,  # verify
    population = site,
    region = "W",
    country = "usa",
    project = "RACA",
    taxon_capture = "rana_cascadae") %>%
  filter(!is.na(bd_its_copies))

raca_valid = raca_clean %>%
  left_join(env_agg %>%
              select(site,
                     date,
                     precip_mm_30_day_sum,
                     temp_c_30_day_mean), by = c("site", "date")) %>%
  select(taxon_capture,
         date,
         site,
         population,
         bd_its_copies,
         life_stage_simple,
         life_stage,
         sex,
         svl_mm,
         precip_mm_30_day_sum,
         temp_c_30_day_mean,
         region,
         country,
         project)

peace = janitor::get_dupes(env_agg, site, date)



```

# RAMU/RASI
```{r}
ramurasi_raw = read.csv(here("data", paste0("sn_res_traj_bd_model_data_2026-03-16.csv")))
```

## Model fitting
```{r}
#| eval: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show model fitting code"

fit_01 <- brm(
  formula  =  bf(
    bd_its_copies ~ s(svl_mm) * taxon_capture,           # mu (log-mean of positive part)
    hu    ~ s(svl_mm) * taxon_capture           # hurdle probability (P zero)
  ),
  data     = raca_clean,
  family   = hurdle_lognormal(),
  chains   = 4,
  iter     = 2000,
  warmup   = 1000,
  cores    = 4,
  seed     = 42,
  prior    = c(
    prior(normal(0, 2), class = b),           # spline coefs for mu
    prior(normal(0, 2), class = b, dpar = hu)    # spline coefs for hu
  )
)


```

# visualize results
```{r}
library(tidybayes)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)  # for combining plots

# Create a prediction grid over the range of svl_mm
svl_seq <- data.frame(svl_mm = seq(min(raca_clean$svl_mm), 
                                    max(raca_clean$svl_mm), 
                                    length.out = 200))

# --- Mu (log-mean of positive part) ---
pred_mu <- fitted(fit_01, 
                  newdata  = svl_seq, 
                  dpar     = "mu",
                  scale    = "response",
                  summary  = TRUE) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(svl_seq)

p_mu <- ggplot(pred_mu, aes(x = svl_mm)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q2.5, ymax = Q97.5), alpha = 0.3, fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(aes(y = Estimate), color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Smooth effect on mu",
       x = "SVL (mm)", y = "mu (log-mean of positive values)") +
  theme_classic()

# --- Hu (hurdle / P(zero)) ---
pred_hu <- fitted(fit_01, 
                  newdata  = svl_seq, 
                  dpar     = "hu",
                  scale    = "response",
                  summary  = TRUE) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  bind_cols(svl_seq) %>%
  mutate(prevalence = 1 - plogis(Estimate),
         prevQ2.5 = 1 - plogis(Q2.5),
         prevQ97.5 = 1 - plogis(Q97.5))

p_hu <- ggplot(pred_hu, aes(x = svl_mm)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q2.5, ymax = Q97.5), alpha = 0.3, fill = "coral") +
  geom_line(aes(y = Estimate), color = "coral", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(title = "Smooth effect on hu",
       x = "SVL (mm)", y = "P(zero)") +
  theme_classic()

p_prev <- ggplot(pred_hu, aes(x = svl_mm)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = prevQ2.5, ymax = prevQ97.5), alpha = 0.3, fill = "coral") +
  geom_line(aes(y = prevalence), color = "coral", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(title = "Smooth effect on hu",
       x = "SVL (mm)", y = "P(zero)") +
  theme_classic()

# Combine
p_mu / p_prev


```
